title: Motion Capture Director Studio
description: The user plays as a motion capture studio director. CesiumMan performs walking animations on a virtual stage while the facecap head floats on a monitor screen showing a live facial performance preview. The user must coordinate the body actor's path and the facial expression intensity to produce a combined performance take, then review and approve the final result.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a motion capture director studio simulation. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader: place CesiumMan on a flat stage platform (a gray plane with grid lines) and mount the facecap head inside a floating rectangular 'monitor' frame positioned upper-right of the scene. Setup: Use a perspective camera at position (0, 2, 5) looking at the stage center. Add ambient light (intensity 0.5), a directional light simulating studio spotlights, and a subtle blue-tinted hemisphere light. Draw a dark studio environment with a ground plane. Play CesiumMan's walk animation using AnimationMixer. For facecap, cycle through available morph targets to simulate facial expressions over time. Interactions (5 steps): Step 1 - Click a 'Start Session' HTML button to begin CesiumMan's walk animation along a circular path on the stage using sin/cos positioning over time. Step 2 - Use a slider labeled 'Expression Intensity' (range 0 to 1) that scales all facecap morph target influences proportionally, visually changing the face from neutral to exaggerated expressions. Step 3 - Click waypoint buttons ('Path A' / 'Path B' / 'Path C') that change CesiumMan's walking trajectory: circular, figure-eight, or straight line with turnaround. Step 4 - Press a 'Record Take' button that captures the current settings (path choice and expression intensity) and displays a 5-second countdown overlay, after which both models freeze in their final pose. Step 5 - A 'Review Panel' appears showing the recorded settings as text. The user clicks 'Approve Take' to trigger a green flash overlay and confetti-like particle burst confirming the take is saved. Ensure the monitor frame around facecap rotates slightly to always face the camera using lookAt. Add an HTML overlay panel for all buttons and the slider, styled with dark semi-transparent backgrounds and white text for a studio control room aesthetic.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  sessionActive: boolean,         // whether the motion capture session is running
  cesiumManPositionX: number,         // CesiumMan's current X coordinate on stage
  cesiumManPositionZ: number,         // CesiumMan's current Z coordinate on stage
  pathRadius: number,         // radius of CesiumMan's circular walking path in meters
  expressionIntensity: number,         // facial expression morph target intensity scale (0-1)
  currentPathMode: string,         // active path mode (circularA or circularB)
  animationTimeElapsed: number,         // total animation time elapsed in seconds
  facecapMorphCount: number,         // number of active morph targets on facecap model
  studioLightIntensity: number,         // directional light intensity value
  monitorFrameVisible: boolean,         // whether the facial monitor frame is visible
}
